differential_diagnoses: Metastatic adenocarcinoma (likely lung or pancreatic origin) with marrow infiltration (myelophthisis)
Hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) secondary to underlying malignancy
Acute myeloid leukemia or other primary hematologic malignancy
Disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC) secondary to malignancy
Severe vitamin/mineral deficiency or toxic bone marrow suppression
Occult infection (e.g., disseminated tuberculosis or fungal infection) causing granulomatous bone marrow infiltration
next_steps: Perform urgent bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and cultures
Calculate H-score for potential HLH and order soluble IL-2 receptor, triglyceride, and fibrinogen levels
Perform image-guided biopsy of the most accessible suspicious lesion (lung or hepatic nodules)
Initiate supportive care with transfusion support for cytopenias
Consult Palliative Care for goals-of-care discussion given high likelihood of advanced metastatic disease
Discontinue unnecessary corticosteroids and schedule diagnostic imaging (PET-CT) for staging
Evaluate etiology of pancreatitis and liver enzyme elevations
